title: Contact & Collaborate
text:
I am always glad to hear from researchers, clinicians, and students interested in neuroscience, machine learning, or neurotechnology education. Please reach out by email or connect with me on LinkedIn.

Email: jana.mahmoud@mail.utoronto.ca
LinkedIn: linkedin.com/in/jana0511

If you are affiliated with the Krembil Brain Institute, NeurotechUofT, or a related research or clinical program and would like to collaborate, feel free to get in touch directly.
I would also love to mentor or be mentored, and I am open to freelance work in tutoring, coaching, advising, web development, marketing, research support, manuscript editing, and peer reviewing.

title: CRANIA Conference
text: Thank you for stopping by my poster today!

title: CRANIA Conference
text:
Connect With Me
I am a recent Honours BSc Neuroscience graduate and incoming MHSc student in the Translational Research Program at the University of Toronto, currently working in the Hodaie Lab at the Krembil Brain Institute. My research applies machine learning and radiomic analysis to predict surgical outcomes in trigeminal neuralgia. I am always glad to discuss this work, related methods, or potential collaboration, and I would welcome the chance to connect with Principal Investigators, clinicians, and researchers working in adjacent areas.
Email: jana.mahmoud@mail.utoronto.ca | LinkedIn: linkedin.com/in/jana0511

title: CRANIA Conference
text:
Connect With NeurotechUofT
Beyond my own research, I serve as Co-President of Operations for NeurotechUofT, the University of Toronto’s undergraduate neuroengineering design team. We work across neuroscience, software, hardware, education, and research portfolios, and we actively benefit from Principal Investigator support and partnership on R&D projects.
We are currently building a new collaboration with Dr. Taufik Valiante and CRANIA Lab, and recently partnered with Dr. Hans Katzberg on our Post-Stroke Rehabilitation (PSR) project:

“We’re excited to announce a new collaboration with Dr. Hans Katzberg for the Post-Stroke Rehabilitation project! Through research and clinical care, HDK Neuromuscular aims to improve daily lives of patients living with neuromuscular disease. We’re thrilled to be working together on upcoming initiatives for the PSR project!”

Dr. Katzberg’s support included $2,000 in funding to help advance this work.
If you are a Principal Investigator or researcher interested in partnering with NeurotechUofT on an R&D project, we would love to connect. Book a 20-minute coffee chat with me and the NeurotechUofT team below, or visit our website to learn more.







Official NeurotechUofT Website


If the page above doesn’t load, you can visit it directly at neurotechuoft.ca/about.

title: Research
text:
Presenting “Machine Learning-Based Prediction of Pain Relief Following Surgery for Trigeminal Neuralgia” at CAN-ACN 2026.



Current Project
Machine Learning-Based Prediction of Pain Recurrence Following Trigeminal Neuralgia Surgery Hodaie Lab, Toronto Western Hospital Krembil Brain Institute | Sept 2025 – Apr 2026 Principal Investigator: Mojgan Hodaie, MD, MSc, FRCSC | Supervisor: Jerry Li, PhD Candidate
Curated a multimodal dataset of 350+ trigeminal neuralgia patients from the TWH facial pain clinic, extracting clinical variables from EPIC EMR. Developed machine learning classification models (XGBoost, SHAP) for individualized pain-free survival estimation and recurrence prediction, using PyRadiomics for radiomic feature extraction from high-resolution MRI and SHAP-based interpretability analyses to identify key predictive biomarkers.


Past Projects
Pregnancy During the COVID-19 Pandemic (PdCP) Associated with Altered Infant Brain Connectivity Manning Lab, Hospital for Sick Children | January 2026
Led development of a multimodal neuroimaging pipeline linking DTI-based structural connectivity with resting-state functional connectivity to examine how prenatal COVID-19 exposure alters white-matter microstructure and functional coupling in the developing brain.
Investigating the Representational Transformations Underlying the Learning of Exceptions in Visual Categories Mack Lab, Center for Biological Timing & Cognition | Jan 2023 – Aug 2025
Managed end-to-end participant coordination across concurrent fMRI study cohorts and conducted quality control review of 70+ MRI scans at the Toronto Neuroimaging Facility (ToNI).
Utilizing Dragonfly ORS AI Tools for Improved Rabbit Lung Micro-CT Scan Image Segmentation in VILI/ARDS Analysis Zhang Lab, St. Michael’s Hospital, Unity Health Toronto | Sept 2023 – Apr 2024
Analyzed 100+ micro-CT scan files and implemented Dragonfly ORS and FreeSurfer software for automated lung segmentation, comparing manual and automated segmentation accuracy.
ANGLER-FISH: An In Situ Approach to Resolve Related mRNAs in Neuronal Wiring and Cell Transplantation Models Lefebvre Lab, Hospital for Sick Children | May – Sept 2023
Medicine by Design Program, SSuRE Program ($10,000 Studentship Award). Implemented the ANGLER-FISH pipeline for oligonucleotide probe design and FISH labeling to map related mRNA transcripts in CNS tissues.


Manuscript in Preparation

Mahmoud, J., Li, J., Garcia-Flores, E., Wolfensohn, A., Hodaie, M. Machine Learning-Based Prediction of Pain Recurrence Following Trigeminal Neuralgia Surgery.



Presentations & Posters

Mahmoud, J., Li, J., Garcia-Flores, E., Wolfensohn, A., Hodaie, M. Machine Learning-Based Prediction of Pain Recurrence Following Trigeminal Neuralgia Surgery. CRANIA Conference 2026. Upcoming July 2026. (Poster)

Mahmoud, J., Li, J. Machine Learning-Based Prediction of Pain Recurrence Following Trigeminal Neuralgia Surgery. Trinity College Undergraduate Research Conference (TCURC). March 2026. (Oral)

Mahmoud, J., Lu, C., Zhang, H. Bioimaging & Molecular Insights: Applications in Neuroscience Research. ASSU Research Conference. 2024. (Oral)
Mahmoud, J., Lu, C., Zhang, H. AI-Based Lung Segmentation in Preclinical Imaging. UofT ROP Poster Symposium. March 2024. (Poster)

A full CV can be shared upon request or as part of a specific application. Feel free to reach out on the Contact & Collaborate page.

title: Leadership
text:
Co-President, Operations, NeurotechUofT Faculty of Applied Sciences & Engineering | June 2026 – Present
Leading operations for the University of Toronto’s only undergraduate neuroengineering design team, coordinating planning and accountability across neuroscience, software, hardware, education, community, and research portfolios. Managing funding and sponsorship strategy, including a new collaboration with Dr. Taufik Valiante and CRANIA Lab, alongside partners at Krembil/UHN, SickKids, and the Institute of Biomedical Engineering.
Executive Advisor, UofT Undergraduate Research Student Association (URSA) Sept 2025 – Apr 2026
Provided operational guidance across marketing, finance, mentorship, web development, and lab-tour portfolios; served as HR liaison and supported planning for the Tri-Campus Undergraduate Research Conference.
Chief Executive Assistant, Office of the President, UofT Students’ Union (UTSU) Jun 2024 – Apr 2025
Served as primary liaison between the President’s Office and the Executive Committee, coordinating daily operations, calendars, communications, and institutional documentation across a 25+ member executive body. Authored 10+ research-driven white papers and briefing documents, including the Interim Food Insecurity Report. Coordinated development and supporting documentation for the Eat after Eight Program, a campus-wide initiative addressing food insecurity through extended dining hours and meal support, and organized the Survivor-Centric Peer Support Sessions initiative in collaboration with The Pears Project and the Dandelion Initiative.
Vice President of Events, UofT Pre-Medical Student Association (UTPMSA) Aug 2022 – Apr 2025
Organized and executed major events including Club Fair, Program Fair, the Temerty Faculty of Medicine Information Session, and the annual Medical Symposium, serving 200–400+ attendees per event. Secured more than $10,000 in sponsorships.
Hackathon Director & Advisor, UTRAHacks UofT Robotics Association (UTRA) | Jul 2023 – Jun 2024
Directed a 12-person team delivering a 450+ participant event, securing more than $17,000 in sponsorships and managing external media and promotional outreach with the Faculty of Applied Science & Engineering, U of T Robotics Institute, and Major League Hacking.
Lead Peer Mentor, Trinity College Life Sciences First-Year Community Aug 2023 – Apr 2025
Designed and led weekly structured learning sessions and one-on-one support for 30 first-year Life Sciences students across two consecutive cohorts, functioning as a primary instructor for academic transition content.
Registered Study Group Leader, Academic Success Centre Ran weekly collaborative review sessions for PSL300H1, BIO230H1, JHA410H1, and HMB204H1, developing practice problems and supporting students in real time.
Patient Visitor Volunteer, Toronto Western Hospital, University Health Network Feb 2026 – Present
Support inpatient experience by providing companionship, conversation, and patient-centred social interaction; assist medically stable patients with supervised walks and liaise with nursing and clinical staff on patient and visitor concerns.
Head Swim Instructor & Lifeguard, UofT Hart House Fitness & Aquatics 2022 – 2024
Planned and led swim lessons for adults and seniors, focusing on water safety and skill development across various ability levels. Conducted skill evaluations and implemented accident prevention protocols in accordance with Ontario safety regulations.
Hospital Volunteer, North York General Hospital July – Sept 2022
Provided wayfinding assistance to patients and families navigating the facility and locating clinics, and supported patient intake processes at the General site.
Intramural Athletics, University of Toronto Oct 2022 – Mar 2023
Participated in Badminton and Ultimate Frisbee intramural teams, contributing to team performance and campus community engagement.

title: Jana Mahmoud, HBSc
text:
I am an incoming MHSc student in the Translational Research Program at the Temerty Faculty of Medicine, University of Toronto, and a recent Honours BSc Neuroscience graduate from the University of Toronto.
My research is conducted in the Hodaie Lab at the Krembil Brain Institute, Toronto Western Hospital, under the supervision of Dr. Mojgan Hodaie, where I work on machine learning-based prediction of surgical outcomes in trigeminal neuralgia. I am also Co-President of Operations for NeurotechUofT, the University of Toronto’s undergraduate neuroengineering design team, currently building a new collaboration with Dr. Taufik Valiante and CRANIA Lab at Krembil Brain Institute.
In my spare time, I am an avid café hopper always chasing a good matcha, a photography enthusiast, an occasional sketcher, and someone who loves a good back-and-forth discussion as much as a quiet walk near water.

Education

MHSc, Translational Research Program, Temerty Faculty of Medicine, University of Toronto | 2026 – 2028
Honours BSc, Neuroscience (Minors: Psychology & Physiology), University of Toronto | 2022 – 2026
Lab2Market Discover National Program, Aging and Brain Health Cohort | 2026

A full CV can be shared upon request or as part of a specific application. Feel free to reach out on the Contact & Collaborate page.
